cases.doc: Library is already registered with the endpoint
cases.name: AlreadyRegistered

cases.doc: Compose message already exists for this GUID and index
cases.name: ComposeExists

cases.doc: Compose message not found for the given GUID and index
cases.name: ComposeNotFound

cases.doc: Default receive library is not set for the source endpoint
cases.name: DefaultReceiveLibUnavailable

cases.doc: Default send library is not set for the destination endpoint
cases.name: DefaultSendLibUnavailable

cases.doc: Supplied native token fee is less than required
cases.name: InsufficientNativeFee

cases.doc: Supplied ZRO token fee is less than required
cases.name: InsufficientZroFee

cases.doc: Timeout expiry is invalid (already expired)
cases.name: InvalidExpiry

cases.doc: Amount is invalid (negative)
cases.name: InvalidAmount

cases.doc: Compose index exceeds maximum allowed value
cases.name: InvalidIndex

cases.doc: Nonce is invalid for the requested operation
cases.name: InvalidNonce

cases.doc: Payload hash is invalid (empty hash not allowed)
cases.name: InvalidPayloadHash

cases.doc: Receive library is not valid for the receiver and source endpoint
cases.name: InvalidReceiveLibrary

cases.doc: Operation requires a non-default (custom) library
cases.name: OnlyNonDefaultLib

cases.doc: Library must support receiving messages
cases.name: OnlyReceiveLib

cases.doc: Library must be registered with the endpoint
cases.name: OnlyRegisteredLib

cases.doc: Library must support sending messages
cases.name: OnlySendLib

cases.doc: Messaging path cannot be initialized for the given origin
cases.name: PathNotInitializable

cases.doc: Message cannot be verified for the given origin
cases.name: PathNotVerifiable

cases.doc: Payload hash does not match the stored hash
cases.name: PayloadHashNotFound

cases.doc: New value is the same as existing value
cases.name: SameValue

cases.doc: Caller is not authorized (not OApp or delegate)
cases.name: Unauthorized

cases.doc: Endpoint ID is not supported by the library
cases.name: UnsupportedEid

cases.doc: ZRO fee must be greater than zero when pay_in_zro is true
cases.name: ZeroZroFee

cases.doc: ZRO token address is not set
cases.name: ZroUnavailable

udt_struct_v0.doc: Parameters for sending a cross-chain message.
udt_struct_v0.name: MessagingParams

fields.doc: Destination endpoint ID (chain identifier).
fields.name: dst_eid

fields.doc: The message payload to send.
fields.name: message

fields.doc: Encoded executor and DVN options.
fields.name: options

fields.doc: Whether to pay fees in ZRO token instead of native token.
fields.name: pay_in_zro

fields.doc: Receiver address on the destination chain (32 bytes).
fields.name: receiver

udt_struct_v0.doc: Source message information identifying where a cross-chain message came from.
udt_struct_v0.name: Origin

fields.doc: Nonce for this pathway.
fields.name: nonce

fields.doc: Sender address on the source chain (32 bytes).
fields.name: sender

fields.doc: Source endpoint ID (chain identifier).
fields.name: src_eid

fields.doc: Fee paid in native token (XLM).
fields.name: native_fee

fields.doc: Fee paid in ZRO token (LayerZero token).
fields.name: zro_fee

udt_struct_v0.doc: Receipt returned after successfully sending a cross-chain message.
udt_struct_v0.name: MessagingReceipt

fields.doc: The fees charged for sending the message.
fields.name: fee

fields.doc: Globally unique identifier for the message.
fields.name: guid

fields.doc: The outbound nonce for this pathway.
fields.name: nonce

udt_union_v0.doc: Type of message library indicating supported operations.
udt_union_v0.name: MessageLibType

void_v0.doc: Supports both sending and receiving messages.
void_v0.name: SendAndReceive

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Version information for a message library.

Note: `minor` and `endpoint_version` use `u32` instead of `u8` because Stellar does not
support `u8` types in contract interface functions.
udt_struct_v0.name: MessageLibVersion

fields.doc: Endpoint version (should not exceed u8::MAX = 255).
fields.name: endpoint_version

fields.doc: Major version number.
fields.name: major

fields.doc: Minor version number (should not exceed u8::MAX = 255).
fields.name: minor

udt_struct_v0.doc: Timeout configuration for receive library transitions.
udt_struct_v0.name: Timeout

fields.doc: Unix timestamp in seconds when the timeout expires.
fields.name: expiry

fields.doc: The old library address that remains valid during the grace period.
fields.name: lib

udt_struct_v0.doc: Parameters for setting message library configuration.
udt_struct_v0.name: SetConfigParam

fields.doc: XDR-encoded configuration data.
fields.name: config

fields.doc: The type of configuration (e.g., executor, ULN).
fields.name: config_type

fields.doc: The endpoint ID this config applies to.
fields.name: eid

udt_struct_v0.doc: Resolved library information with default status.
udt_struct_v0.name: ResolvedLibrary

fields.doc: Whether this is the default library (true) or OApp-specific (false).
fields.name: is_default

fields.doc: The resolved library address.
fields.name: lib

udt_struct_v0.doc: Outbound packet containing all information for cross-chain transmission.
udt_struct_v0.name: OutboundPacket

fields.doc: Destination endpoint ID.
fields.name: dst_eid

fields.doc: Globally unique identifier for this message.
fields.name: guid

fields.doc: The message payload.
fields.name: message

fields.doc: Outbound nonce for this pathway.
fields.name: nonce

fields.doc: Receiver address on destination chain (32 bytes).
fields.name: receiver

fields.doc: Sender address on source chain.
fields.name: sender

fields.doc: Source endpoint ID.
fields.name: src_eid

udt_struct_v0.doc: A fee recipient with the amount to be paid.
udt_struct_v0.name: FeeRecipient

fields.doc: Amount of fee to pay.
fields.name: amount

fields.doc: The address to send the fee to.
fields.name: to

udt_struct_v0.doc: Result of send operation containing fees and encoded packet.
udt_struct_v0.name: FeesAndPacket

fields.doc: The encoded packet ready for transmission.
fields.name: encoded_packet

fields.doc: List of native token fee recipients (executor, DVNs, treasury).
fields.name: native_fee_recipients

fields.doc: List of ZRO token fee recipients (treasury).
fields.name: zro_fee_recipients

function_v0.doc:
Sends a composed message from an OApp to a composer.

The composer MUST assert the sender because anyone can send compose msg with this function.
With the same GUID, the OApp can send compose to multiple composers at the same time.
function_v0.name: send_compose

function_v0.doc: Clears a composed message by the composer.\n\nThis is a PULL mode versus the PUSH mode of `lz_compose`.
function_v0.name: clear_compose

function_v0.doc: Emits an alert event when `lz_compose` execution fails.\n\nCalled by the executor to notify about failed compose message delivery attempts.
function_v0.name: lz_compose_alert

function_v0.doc: Returns the stored hash for a composed message, or `None` if not queued.
function_v0.name: compose_queue

function_v0.doc: Skips the next expected inbound nonce without verifying.\n\nThe nonce to skip must be the next expected nonce.
function_v0.name: skip

function_v0.doc: Marks a packet as verified, but disallows execution until it is re-verified.\n\nRequires the payload hash not been executed.
function_v0.name: nilify

function_v0.doc: Marks a nonce as unexecutable and un-verifiable. The nonce can never be re-verified or executed.
function_v0.name: burn

function_v0.doc: Generates the next GUID for an outbound packet.
function_v0.name: next_guid

function_v0.doc: Returns the current outbound nonce for a specific destination.
function_v0.name: outbound_nonce

function_v0.doc:
Returns the max index of the longest gapless sequence of verified message nonces.

The uninitialized value is 0. The first nonce is always 1.

Note: OApp explicitly skipped nonces count as "verified" for these purposes.
function_v0.name: inbound_nonce

function_v0.doc: Returns the pending inbound nonces for a specific path.
function_v0.name: pending_inbound_nonces

function_v0.doc: Returns the payload hash for a specific inbound nonce.
function_v0.name: inbound_payload_hash

function_v0.doc: Registers a new message library with the endpoint.
function_v0.name: register_library

function_v0.doc: Sets the default send library for a destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: set_default_send_library

function_v0.doc:
Sets the default receive library for a source endpoint.

If a grace period is provided and there was a previous library, the old library
remains valid until the grace period expires.
function_v0.name: set_default_receive_library

function_v0.doc:
Sets or removes the default receive library timeout for a source endpoint.

If a timeout is provided, it must be valid and not expired. If no timeout is provided,
the default receive library timeout is removed.
function_v0.name: set_default_receive_lib_timeout

function_v0.doc: Sets or removes a custom send library for an OApp to a specific destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: set_send_library

function_v0.doc:
Sets or removes a custom receive library for an OApp to a specific source endpoint.

If a grace period is provided and there was a previous library, the old library
remains valid until the grace period expires.
function_v0.name: set_receive_library

function_v0.doc:
Sets or removes the custom receive library timeout for an OApp.

Allows an OApp to extend or remove the validity period of a previously set library
after switching to a new one.
function_v0.name: set_receive_library_timeout

function_v0.doc: Sets the configuration for a message library.\n\nRequires the caller to be the OApp or its delegate and the library to be registered.
function_v0.name: set_config

function_v0.doc: Checks if a message library is registered.
function_v0.name: is_registered_library

function_v0.doc: Returns the index of a message library.
function_v0.name: get_library_index

function_v0.doc: Returns the number of registered message libraries.
function_v0.name: registered_libraries_count

function_v0.doc: Returns a list of registered message libraries within the specified range.
function_v0.name: get_registered_libraries

function_v0.doc: Checks if an endpoint ID is supported.\nReturns true only if both the default send/receive libraries are set for the given eid
function_v0.name: is_supported_eid

function_v0.doc: Returns the default send library for a destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: default_send_library

function_v0.doc: Returns the default receive library for a source endpoint.
function_v0.name: default_receive_library

function_v0.doc: Returns the default receive library timeout for a source endpoint.
function_v0.name: default_receive_library_timeout

function_v0.doc: Returns the effective send library for an OApp and destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: get_send_library

function_v0.doc: Returns the effective receive library for an OApp and source endpoint.
function_v0.name: get_receive_library

function_v0.doc: Returns the receive library timeout for an OApp and source endpoint.
function_v0.name: receive_library_timeout

function_v0.doc: Checks if a receive library is valid for an OApp and source endpoint.
function_v0.name: is_valid_receive_library

function_v0.doc: Returns the configuration for a message library for a specific endpoint ID and configuration type.
function_v0.name: get_config

function_v0.doc: Quotes the messaging fee for sending a cross-chain message.
function_v0.name: quote

function_v0.doc: Sends a cross-chain message to a destination endpoint.\n\nOApp sender needs to transfer the fees to the endpoint before sending the message
function_v0.name: send

function_v0.doc: Verifies an inbound cross-chain message from a configured receive library.
function_v0.name: verify

function_v0.doc: Clears a verified message from the endpoint.\n\nThis is a PULL mode versus the PUSH mode of `lz_receive`.
function_v0.name: clear

function_v0.doc: Emits an alert event when `lz_receive` execution fails.\n\nCalled by the executor to notify about failed message delivery attempts.
function_v0.name: lz_receive_alert

function_v0.doc: Sets the ZRO token address for fee payments.
function_v0.name: set_zro

function_v0.doc: Sets or removes a delegate address that can act on behalf of the OApp.
function_v0.name: set_delegate

function_v0.doc: Returns the endpoint ID.
function_v0.name: eid

function_v0.doc: Checks if a messaging path can be/has been initialized for the given origin and receiver.
function_v0.name: initializable

function_v0.doc: Checks if a message can be verified for the given origin and receiver.
function_v0.name: verifiable

function_v0.doc: Returns the native token address used for fee payments.
function_v0.name: native_token

function_v0.doc: Returns the ZRO token address if set.
function_v0.name: zro

function_v0.doc: Returns the delegate address for an OApp if set.
function_v0.name: delegate

function_v0.doc:
Recovers tokens sent to this contract by mistake.

# Arguments
* `token` - The token address to recover
* `to` - The address to send the token to
* `amount` - The amount to send
function_v0.name: recover_token

function_v0.doc: Returns the current owner address, or None if no owner is set.
function_v0.name: owner

function_v0.doc: Returns the pending owner address for 2-step transfer, or None if no transfer is pending.
function_v0.name: pending_owner

function_v0.doc:
Transfers ownership immediately to a new address.

Use with caution - if you transfer to a wrong address, ownership is lost forever.
Consider using `begin_ownership_transfer` instead.

# Panics
- `OwnerNotSet` if no owner is currently set
- `TransferInProgress` if a 2-step transfer is in progress
function_v0.name: transfer_ownership

function_v0.doc:
Begins an ownership transfer to a new address.

The new owner must call `accept_ownership()` within `ttl` ledgers
to complete the transfer. The pending transfer will automatically expire after.

# Arguments
- `new_owner` - The proposed new owner
- `ttl` - Number of ledgers the new owner has to accept.
Use `0` to cancel a pending transfer (new_owner must match pending).

# Panics
- `OwnerNotSet` if no owner is currently set
- `NoPendingTransfer` when cancelling and no pending transfer exists
- `InvalidTtl` if ttl exceeds max TTL
- `InvalidPendingOwner` when cancelling with wrong new_owner address
function_v0.name: begin_ownership_transfer

function_v0.doc:
Accepts a pending 2-step ownership transfer.

Must be called by the pending owner before the TTL expires.

# Panics
- `NoPendingTransfer` if there is no pending transfer (or it expired)
function_v0.name: accept_ownership

function_v0.doc:
Permanently renounces ownership.

# Panics
- `OwnerNotSet` if no owner is currently set
- `TransferInProgress` if a 2-step transfer is in progress (cancel it first)
function_v0.name: renounce_ownership

function_v0.doc:
Extends the instance TTL.

# Arguments

* `threshold` - The threshold to extend the TTL (if current TTL is below this, extend).
* `extend_to` - The TTL to extend to.
function_v0.name: extend_instance_ttl

function_v0.doc:
Sets TTL configs for instance and persistent storage.

- `None` values remove the corresponding config (disables auto-extension for that type)
- Validates that `threshold <= extend_to <= MAX_TTL`

# Arguments
- `instance` - TTL config for instance storage
- `persistent` - TTL config for persistent storage

# Panics
- `TtlConfigFrozen` if configs are frozen
- `InvalidTtlConfig` if validation fails
function_v0.name: set_ttl_configs

function_v0.doc: Returns the current TTL configs as (instance_config, persistent_config).
function_v0.name: ttl_configs

function_v0.doc:
Permanently freezes TTL configs, preventing any future modifications.

This is irreversible and provides immutability guarantees to users.
Emits `TtlConfigsFrozen` event.

# Panics
- `TtlConfigAlreadyFrozen` if already frozen
function_v0.name: freeze_ttl_configs

function_v0.doc: Returns whether TTL configs are frozen.
function_v0.name: is_ttl_configs_frozen

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when the signature threshold is changed.

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when ownership is transferred (both single-step and two-step completion).
event_v0.name: OwnershipTransferred

event_v0.name: OwnershipTransferring

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when a 2-step ownership transfer is cancelled.
event_v0.name: OwnershipTransferCancelled

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when ownership is renounced.
event_v0.name: OwnershipRenounced

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when a role is granted.
event_v0.name: RoleGranted

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when a role is revoked.
event_v0.name: RoleRevoked

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when a role admin is changed.
event_v0.name: RoleAdminChanged

udt_struct_v0.doc: TTL configuration: threshold (when to extend) and extend_to (target TTL).
udt_struct_v0.name: TtlConfig

fields.doc: Target TTL after extension (in ledgers).
fields.name: extend_to

fields.doc: TTL threshold that triggers extension (in ledgers).
fields.name: threshold

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when TTL configs are set.
event_v0.name: TtlConfigsSet

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when TTL configs are frozen.
event_v0.name: TtlConfigsFrozen
